ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "tweet" TEXT /* Original tweet text */,
  "clean_tweet" TEXT /* Cleaned tweet text (without special characters, etc.) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was posted */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Twitter user who posted the tweet */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the Twitter user who posted the tweet */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the tweet was posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the hashtag belongs to */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag text */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the hashtag in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource (e.g., oxygen, Remdesivir, hospital beds) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the resource */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Available quantity of the resource */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the resource (e.g., liters, units, beds) */,
  " supplier" TEXT /* Supplier of the resource */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the request belongs to */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource requested */,
  "requested_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the resource requested */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., pending, fulfilled, rejected) */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the request */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Date and time the request was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_requests_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_requests_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., healthcare professional, administrator) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who has the permission */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource the permission applies to */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action the user is allowed to perform on the resource (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_resources" (
  "tweet_resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet-resource pair */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that mentions the resource */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource mentioned in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_resources_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_resources_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_entities" (
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each entity */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that contains the entity */,
  "entity_type" TEXT /* Type of entity (e.g., URL, emoji) */,
  "entity_text" TEXT /* Text of the entity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_entities_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_allocation" (
  "allocation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each allocation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who was allocated the resource */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource that was allocated */,
  "allocated_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the resource allocated */,
  "allocation_date" TEXT /* Date the resource was allocated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allocation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_allocation_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_allocation_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES resources ("resource_id")
)
